figures.caption: Figure 8: APEX-EM Plan Graph. Top layer: committed plans as nodes with inter-plan edges encoding semantic similarity, structural overlap, derivation, and supersession. Bottom layer: Entity Graph ontology. Blue dash-dot edges connect plans to the entities and procedural steps they reference, enabling entity-graph-based retrieval. Plan P3 (failed, ×) is retained as a negative example with error annotations.

Figure 9: BCB iteration reduction from E1 to E10 across all ablation configurations. Despite the limited dynamic range (max_iterations=3), all configs show a clear shift toward first-attempt solutions.
Figure 10: KGQA iteration reduction from E1 to E10. With max_iterations=10, the shift is dramatic: firstattempt solutions nearly double while tasks exhausting all iterations are halved.

Figure 5: BCB Entity Graph. Tasks A (“filter and group CSV data”) and B (“compute rolling average of stock prices”) share entities (pandas, DataFrame) and procedural steps, enabling structural retrieval despite different descriptions.
Table 7: ALFWorld (Shridhar et al., 2021) embodied navigation results. MemRL baselines from Wang et al. (2025) with GPT-5-mini backbone. APEX-EM experiments planned.
Table 6: Lifelong Agent Bench (Zheng et al., 2025) results: OS interaction and database tasks. MemRL baselines from Wang et al. (2025) with GPT-4o-mini backbone. APEX-EM experiments planned.

Figure 6: KGQA Entity Graph. TaskEntities are Wikidata entities with QIDs; TaskProperties are Wikidata properties with PIDs. Tasks C and D produce identical structural signatures despite different entities (Curry vs LeBron), enabling cross-entity structural retrieval. The EQUIVALENT_TO edge (cyan) links entities that serve analogous roles across tasks.

Table 5: Multi-domain knowledge reasoning results on HLE (Phan et al., 2025) (500-question ablation subset). MemRL baselines from Wang et al. (2025) with Gemini3-pro backbone. All APEX-EM ablations complete (10 epochs). Full 2,500-question evaluation running separately (E2: 21.2%).
Table 1: Condensed Experience ontology. Each Experience node in the PKG stores the following layered structure. Layers are independently queryable.

Table 11: Iteration count reduction from epoch 1 to epoch 10. As memory accumulates, the agent requires fewer self-correction iterations to solve tasks. BCB reduction is modest (11–17%) due to max_iterations=3; KGQA shows dramatic reduction (37–43%) with max_iterations=10. On BCB, A3 (binary signal) shows the largest reduction, suggesting iteration does more “heavy lifting” when feedback quality is lower. On KGQA, A5 (Opus judge) leads, indicating higherquality feedback produces more effective memory entries.

Table 3: Code generation results on BigCodeBench (Full, 1,140 tasks, 7:3 train/transfer split, seed=42). Last Epoch Success Rate / Cumulative Success Rate on train split (798 tasks). MemRL baselines from Wang et al. (2025) with GPT-4o backbone. All APEX-EM configs complete (10 epochs).

Figure 7: HLE Entity Graph. TaskEntities are mathematical concepts; TaskProperties are mathematical operations. Tasks E (Spin bordism) and F (homotopy groups) share the same procedural pattern and the Atiyah-Hirzebruch spectral sequence entity, enabling structural retrieval across different algebraic topology problems.

Figure 1: The PRGII workflow. A task t flows left-to-right through five phases: Plan (task understanding, entity/schema discovery, structural signature extraction), Retrieve (semantic search, structural signature matching, and PKG traversal with dual-track separation into positive P+ and negative P− examples), Generate (artifact generation conditioned on P(t), P+, P−, with reflection layers steering generation), Iterate (execute, validate via Vd, refine on failure), and Ingest (Teacher evaluation, quality gate, commit to Experience Memory M via Add or Merge). The PKG supports entity resolution during planning and experience retrieval during retrieval. Both entity and experience nodes are updated during ingestion, closing the online learning loop.

Table 10: Cross-task transfer results with external baselines. BCB: 342 unseen tasks (30% held-out, seed=42); KGQA: 1,079 unseen questions (KGQAGen-10k test split); Lifelong-OS/DB and ALFWorld: held-out splits from Wang et al. (2025). Memory is frozen from training (no new learning). ∆ = pp improvement over respective no-memory baseline. †MemRL baselines (Wang et al., 2025); BCB uses GPT-4o, Lifelong uses GPT-4o-mini, ALFWorld uses GPT-5-mini. ‡KGQAGen baselines (Zhang et al., 2025a) report LASM Accuracy on the 1,079- question test split; “w/ SP” = oracle supporting subgraph. TK = experiments planned.
